pi_items.source: Task description specifies this name for the new store
Task description specifies this email for the European store
Task description specifies Euro (EUR) as the default currency for the new European store
Task description specifies United Kingdom address with country code GB
Task description specifies London as the locality in the store address
Confirmed in /workspace/scripts/seed_products.php line 45. Product is created with this exact title in the Drupal database during setup.

Examined /workspace/scripts/seed_products.php which is the authoritative product seed script. Line 47 defines: ["title" => "Samsung 65-inch 4K QLED Smart TV", ...]. There is NO product named 'Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra' in the seed data. The export_result.sh (line 99) searches for 'Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra' which will NOT match the actual database product.

Confirmed in /workspace/scripts/seed_products.php line 49. Full product title is 'Logitech MX Master 3S Wireless Mouse'. The metadata key value 'Logitech MX Master 3S' matches the product via LIKE search pattern used in export_result.sh.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a European storefront for 'Urban Electronics' with store name 'Urban Electronics Europe', email 'europe@urbanelectronics.com', currency EUR, and location London, GB. Two of the three products to assign are correctly specified: Sony WH-1000XM5 Wireless Headphones and Logitech MX Master 3S Wireless Mouse both exist in the seed database. However, the metadata lists 'Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra' as a product to assign, but the actual seeded product is 'Samsung 65-inch 4K QLED Smart TV' - this is a critical mismatch that will cause the product assignment verification to fail.
